summary: sensivity ships stealth game-cheat with Windows persistence, eval'd obfuscated payload, and process-impersonation
details: On require() or invocation of the `sensivity` bin, launcher.js performs a chain of hostile actions on Windows installers: (1) writes a Run-key persistence entry under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run named 'OneDriveUpdate' that points at OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs, which in turn launches `node launcher.js` hidden — both the registry name and VBS filename are crafted to impersonate Microsoft OneDrive so users cannot trace the persistence to this package; (2) spawns a detached supervisor process with stdio:'ignore' and windowsHide:true, then process.exit(0)s the parent, leaving a hidden long-running supervisor + worker that respawn each other and bind a TCP listener on:3000 — surviving the importing process's exit; (3) sets process.title='Runtime Broker' on both supervisor and worker to masquerade as the legitimate Windows RuntimeBroker.exe in Task Manager; (4) eval()s server.obf.js, an 88KB obfuscator.io-style RC4-encoded blob with 1400+ deobfuscation transforms, deliberately concealing the package's real logic; (5) loads a 6.5MB Windows PE native module sens.node whose strings ('LeftFoot','LeftHand','Head','Teleport') and bundled UI ('Aimbot','Triggerbot','ESP','Radar','Vehicle Spawn') identify it as an FPS game cheat — published to npm under the vague description 'Sensivity Control Panel'; (6) wraps console.log/warn/error to swallow lines containing 'program module loaded' and string-replace 'sens.node' with 'program module' and the absolute APP_DIR with '<app>' to scrub the package's identifying strings from any logs; (7) polls Get-Process every 3s for chrome/msedge/opera/opera_gx/brave and reads MainWindowTitle looking for 'YouTube'. The combination of registry persistence + process impersonation + detached background process + eval of obfuscated payload + opaque native binary + log self-erasure is unambiguously hostile.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
